titleStmt.title:
Memorandum by Lieutenant George M. Elsey of the White House Map Room to the Acting Secretary of State
Text transmitted to Secretary Stettinius in Mexico City in telegram 388, February 23, 11 p.m., not printed.

head.hi: Memorandum by Lieutenant George M. Elsey of the White House Map Room to the Acting Secretary of State
head.note: Text transmitted to Secretary Stettinius in Mexico City in telegram 388, February 23, 11 p.m., not printed.

div.p:
The White House Map Room has received the following message from the President, dated 21 February, 6:45 p.m., EWT:Eastern War Time.
“From the President for the Secretary of State, Information: the Acting Secretary.
“In reply to your message dated 20 February from Guatemala CityTelegram 128, p. 75. and the Acting Secretary’s message of the same dayUnnumbered radiogram, p. 76. on the subject of invitations to the United Nations Conference to be held in San Francisco on 25 April and the release of the text on voting procedure, I will let you make final decision without further reference to me. I prefer that invitations should be issued before March 1.”In radiogram of February 23, Acting Secretary Grew informed President Roosevelt that agreement had been reached to issue the invitations on March 1 and, consequently, a series of steps had been taken, which he enumerated for the latter’s information (500.CC/2–2345).

It should be noted that the President had not received the Acting Secretary’s radiogram of 21 February Ante, p. 81. on the text of the proposed public statement on voting procedure at the time he sent this message.
